description: Shared qualifier types vocabulary for Clinical Data Definitions Model. Qualifier types represent semantically different versions of a data definition that a transformation binding must choose between. They are NOT different physical representations (like PARAM vs PARAMCD) — they are scientifically different values.
designPrinciples: Qualifiers represent choices that change the scientific meaning, not just the physical encoding
A transformation binding MUST specify a qualifier value when the data definition has qualifiers
DC derivation concepts (Change, PeakValue, Flag, etc.) do not have qualifiers — they are outputs, not source selections
The qualifier is resolved at the transformation layer; the implementation layer maps qualifier values to physical columns

IntentType.definition: Whether the value reflects planned assignment or actual receipt

DerivationStatus.definition: Whether the value is protocol-defined or analysis-derived

values.Protocol: As defined in the study protocol
values.Analysis: As derived for analysis purposes (e.g., windowed visits)

ReferenceFrame.definition: Whether timing is relative to an event or absolute calendar

values.Relative: Expressed as days/hours from a reference event

BoundaryType.definition: Whether a date marks the start or end of an interval

values.Start: The beginning of a time interval
values.End: The end of a time interval
